models/marts/order_items.yml.purpose: This file defines the business logic and semantic model for order items, including revenue calculations, food/drink item classification, and related metrics. It serves as a mart layer component that aggregates and structures data about individual items within customer orders for analytical reporting.

macros/cents_to_dollars.sql.purpose: Converts monetary values from cents to dollars format for consistent financial reporting across the organization.

models/marts/orders.sql.purpose: This model creates a comprehensive orders mart by enriching staged order data with summarized item-level metrics and customer ordering sequence information. It determines order characteristics like whether an order contains food or drink items and calculates totals for cost, price, and item counts.

models/marts/orders.yml.purpose: This file defines a data mart for order analytics, containing key order details like totals, timestamps, and item breakdowns (food/drink). It supports downstream reporting through semantic models, metrics, and saved queries for business insights.

macros/generate_schema_name.sql.purpose: Organizes data assets into appropriate database schemas based on resource type and environment for data governance and separation of concerns.

models/marts/order_items.sql.purpose: This model creates a comprehensive view of order items by joining staging tables for orders, products, and supplies to calculate total supply costs per product. It provides enriched order item data including product details, order timestamps, and aggregated supply chain costs for business analysis.

models/marts/customers.yml.purpose: This file defines a customer data mart that aggregates key customer-level metrics including lifetime spending, order counts, and customer type classification. It serves as a centralized source for customer analytics and supports downstream metrics like average order value and lifetime value calculations.

models/marts/customers.sql.purpose: Creates a comprehensive customer analytics dataset that enables business insights into customer purchasing behavior, retention metrics, and lifetime value calculations for marketing and sales strategies.

models/marts/supplies.yml.purpose: Defines the supplies semantic model to enable business users to analyze supply chain metrics by joining supply and product information.

models/staging/stg_orders.yml.purpose: Maintains clean and validated order-level data with financial calculations to ensure data integrity for business reporting and analytics.

models/staging/stg_orders.sql.purpose: This staging model transforms raw e-commerce order data by renaming fields, converting cent values to dollar amounts, and truncating timestamps to daily granularity for downstream analytics use.

models/staging/stg_products.sql.purpose: This staging model transforms raw product data from the e-commerce system into a standardized format suitable for analytics. It cleans and renames fields, converts price units from cents to dollars, and adds boolean flags to classify products as food or drink items.

models/staging/stg_order_items.yml.purpose: Provides cleaned and validated individual food and drink item data from orders to support detailed order analysis and revenue tracking.

models/staging/stg_supplies.sql.purpose: This staging model transforms raw supply data from the ecom source into a standardized format for downstream analytics. It cleans and renames fields, generates a surrogate key, and converts cost values to dollars.

models/marts/metricflow_time_spine.sql.purpose: Generates a comprehensive date dimension table to enable time-based analysis and reporting across all business metrics.

models/marts/locations.yml.purpose: Establishes location dimension definitions for business intelligence reporting, enabling analysis of location performance metrics and temporal trends with standardized geographic identifiers.

models/marts/products.yml.purpose: Defines product dimension metadata to support business intelligence reporting and analysis by categorizing products and enabling metric calculations across product types and pricing.

models/staging/stg_locations.sql.purpose: This staging model transforms raw store data into a standardized format by renaming columns and extracting date components. It serves as an intermediate layer between raw e-commerce store data and downstream analytics models.

models/staging/stg_customers.sql.purpose: This staging model transforms raw customer data by renaming fields to establish consistent business identifiers and terminology for downstream analytics use.

models/staging/stg_order_items.sql.purpose: This staging model transforms raw e-commerce item data into a standardized format by renaming and selecting key fields such as order item ID, order ID, and product ID. It serves as an intermediate layer between raw data ingestion and downstream analytics or modeling.

models/staging/stg_locations.yml.purpose: Provides a cleaned and standardized list of open locations for business reporting and analysis, ensuring each location has a unique identifier and properly formatted opening date.

models/staging/stg_supplies.yml.purpose: Tracks supply expenses with detailed cost history, enabling financial analysis of procurement spending over time.

models/marts/products.sql.purpose: Provides a cleaned and standardized view of product data for business reporting and analysis.

models/marts/supplies.sql.purpose: Delivers supply chain data in a format suitable for operational reporting and inventory management.

models/staging/stg_customers.yml.purpose: Maintains cleaned customer master data to support customer relationship management and sales analytics.

models/staging/stg_products.yml.purpose: Manages standardized product information for food and beverage items to enable consistent sales and inventory reporting.

models/staging/__sources.yml.purpose: Configures source data definitions and metadata for the e-commerce system's raw data tables.

dbt_project.yml.purpose: This is the main configuration file for a dbt project named 'jaffle_shop' that defines project metadata, file paths, and model configurations. It specifies how data models should be materialized and sets up the project structure for dbt operations.

.pre-commit-config.yaml.purpose: This configuration file defines pre-commit hooks for automated code quality checks and formatting. It specifies repositories and hook IDs that validate YAML files, fix whitespace issues, manage requirements files, and enforce Python code style using Ruff.

models/marts/locations.sql.purpose: Provides a centralized view of location data to support geographic analysis and site-based business operations.

Taskfile.yml.purpose: Defines automated workflows for environment setup, data generation, and database loading operations.

Order Analytics & Revenue.description: Core business domain focused on customer orders, revenue calculations, and item-level profitability analysis for sales reporting and financial insights.

Customer Analytics & Relationship Manage.description: Domain focused on customer behavior analysis, lifetime value calculations, and customer relationship management for marketing and sales strategies.

Product & Supply Chain Management.description: Domain handling product catalog management, supply chain costs, inventory tracking, and product profitability analysis.

Location & Operational Analytics.description: Domain focused on geographic analysis, location performance metrics, and site-based business operations reporting.

Data Infrastructure & Governance.description: Cross-cutting domain providing foundational data infrastructure, time dimension modeling, and data governance capabilities.

Development & Operations Tooling.description: Technical domain containing development workflow automation, code quality tools, and environment management configurations.

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw product data from the e-commerce system into a standardized format suitable for analytics. It cleans and renames fields, converts price units from cents to dollars, and adds boolean flags to classify products as food or drink items.

supporting_evidence.description: Field renaming (sku→product_id, name→product_name), price conversion with cents_to_dollars macro, and boolean logic for food/drink classification
Source reference to ecom.raw_products indicates input dataset
Transformation dbt_model reads source.ecom.raw_products and writes model.stg_products

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw supply data from the ecom source into a standardized format for downstream analytics. It cleans and renames fields, generates a surrogate key, and converts cost values to dollars.

supporting_evidence.description: {{ dbt_utils.generate_surrogate_key(['id', 'sku']) }} as supply_uuid
{{ cents_to_dollars('cost') }} as supply_cost
select * from {{ source('ecom', 'raw_supplies') }}

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw e-commerce order data by renaming fields, converting cent values to dollar amounts, and truncating timestamps to daily granularity for downstream analytics use.

supporting_evidence.description: Field renaming: id → order_id, store_id → location_id, customer → customer_id
Currency conversion from cents to dollars using cents_to_dollars macro
Timestamp truncation using dbt.date_trunc('day','ordered_at')

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This model creates a comprehensive view of order items by joining staging tables for orders, products, and supplies to calculate total supply costs per product. It provides enriched order item data including product details, order timestamps, and aggregated supply chain costs for business analysis.

supporting_evidence.description: Aggregation of supply costs by product_id in order_supplies_summary CTE
Join logic combining order items with orders, products, and supply cost data
Selection of product attributes (product_name, product_price, is_food_item, is_drink_item) indicating business categorization

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This model creates a comprehensive orders mart by enriching staged order data with summarized item-level metrics and customer ordering sequence information. It determines order characteristics like whether an order contains food or drink items and calculates totals for cost, price, and item counts.

supporting_evidence.description: order_items_summary CTE aggregates supply costs, product prices, and counts food/drink items per order
compute_booleans CTE joins orders with item summaries and derives is_food_order/is_drink_order flags
customer_order_count CTE adds customer_order_number using window function ordered by timestamp

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw store data into a standardized format by renaming columns and extracting date components. It serves as an intermediate layer between raw e-commerce store data and downstream analytics models.

supporting_evidence.description: Column renaming operations: id→location_id, name→location_name, tax_rate, and date truncation of opened_at
Source reference to ecom.raw_stores dataset
Transformation dbt_model reads source.ecom.raw_stores and writes model.stg_locations

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw customer data by renaming fields to establish consistent business identifiers and terminology for downstream analytics use.

supporting_evidence.description: id renamed to customer_id and name renamed to customer_name in SELECT clause
Source reference to ecom.raw_customers indicates input dataset
Transformation dbt_model reads source.ecom.raw_customers and writes model.stg_customers

semantic_hotspots.purpose: Converts monetary values from cents to dollars format for consistent financial reporting across the organization.

supporting_evidence.description: Macro definition shows business logic for converting cents to dollars with database-specific implementations
Transformation dbt_model reads none and writes model.cents_to_dollars

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw e-commerce item data into a standardized format by renaming and selecting key fields such as order item ID, order ID, and product ID. It serves as an intermediate layer between raw data ingestion and downstream analytics or modeling.

supporting_evidence.description: Field renaming logic mapping 'id' to 'order_item_id', 'sku' to 'product_id'
Source reference to 'ecom.raw_items' indicates input dataset
Transformation dbt_model reads source.ecom.raw_items and writes model.stg_order_items

semantic_hotspots.purpose: Organizes data assets into appropriate database schemas based on resource type and environment for data governance and separation of concerns.

supporting_evidence.description: Macro implements business rules for schema naming with conditional logic for seeds, custom schemas, and production vs non-production environments
